hypothesis: Incorporating temperature-dependent heating energy (via reactant/product heat capacities) and purification costs estimated from predicted byproduct solubilities into the workflow’s economic metric will reverse the current ranking for Ca2SiO4 synthesis, with Ca3SiO5+SiO2 achieving at least 20% higher grams-per-euro than CaO+SiO2 for 1–5 ton batches sintered at 1300–1500°C.
